topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: The importance of renewable energy sources in combating climate change.
Innovative technologies for solar power generation.
doc2: Strategies for improving energy efficiency in urban areas.
The role of wind energy in the future energy landscape.

topic.name: sustainable urban development
topic.description: The documents discuss strategies and practices for promoting sustainability in urban areas.

doc1: urban planning initiatives to reduce carbon footprint
green building practices in city developments
doc2: the role of public transportation in reducing emissions
doc3: impact of urban green spaces on biodiversity
challenges in implementing sustainable policies in cities

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of solar power on carbon footprint
doc3: policy frameworks for sustainable energy adoption
case studies on community solar projects

topic.name: Medicare and Prescription Drug Policy
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments and acts related to Medicare, focusing on healthcare access, prescription drug pricing, and payment methodologies.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to permit physical therapy to be furnished under the Medicare program to individuals under the care of a dentist.
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to provide for the use of median retail acquisition cost as the basis for Medicaid reimbursement limits on generic drugs.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians to serve hospice patients.
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend for one year the Medicare physician payment rates without change.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise requirements for payments for physician's services to eliminate the sustainable growth rate system.
Document 6: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to require each contract entered into with a prescription drug plan sponsor to prohibit certain contracts with pharmacy benefits managers.
Document 7: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover as durable medical equipment any eye tracking and gaze interaction accessories for speech generating devices.
Document 8: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to eliminate part D cost-sharing for certain non-institutionalized full-benefit dual eligible individuals.
Document 9: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to allow an authorized physician or practitioner who is not enrolled as a Medicare provider to prescribe covered drugs under the Medicare prescription drug benefit.
Document 10: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to the use of average sales price methodology for calculating payments for drugs and biologicals under Medicare part B.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax exemptions, deductions, and credits across different sectors including education, energy, and environmental regulations.

Document 1: exempt from the excise tax on bows and arrows
certain shafts measuring 5/16 of an inch or less in diameter
Document 2: allows the payment of qualified elementary and secondary education expenses from a tax-exempt qualified tuition program
increases from $2,000 to $15,000 the limit on the amount that may be contributed to a tax-exempt Coverdell education savings account
Document 3: deny a tax deduction for the removal costs and damages assessed for a discharge of oil
Document 4: amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the limitation on the number of new qualified hybrid and advanced lean-burn technology vehicles
allow an energy efficient motor vehicles manufacturing tax credit
Document 5: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965
establish teacher education assistance for college and higher education grants
Document 6: increase the rate of such credit from 30% to 50% for hydrogen-related alternative fuel vehicles
extend such credit through 2016 for property related to hydrogen
Document 7: make permanent the taxpayer election to deduct state and local general sales taxes
requires an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $1 million to pay a minimum tax rate of 30%
Document 8: increase the percentage rate of the tax credit for the purchase of alternative fuel motor vehicles
increase and extend funding for such program for FY2010-FY2015
Document 9: allow a tax deduction, up to $10,000 per year, for supplemental education expenses
defines supplemental education expenses as expenses not required for enrollment
Document 10: deny the earned income tax credit to any alien individual who is in an unlawful immigration status
amends the Internal Revenue Code

topic.name: U.S. Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, focusing on the temporary suspension of duties on various imported goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on dry adhesive copolyamide pellets.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain polycrystalline fibers designed for use in pollution control devices for motor vehicles.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain personalized jewelry.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain children's textile upper footwear.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to provide for the duty-free treatment of certain engines to be installed in work trucks.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on ceiling fans for permanent installation.
Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to grant temporarily duty-free treatment of black 820 inkjet printing ink.
Document 9: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on 40% Polymer acid salt/polymer amide 60% Butyl acetate.
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers that are not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.

topic.name: Military and Intelligence Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the oversight, funding, and operational management of military and intelligence activities in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to establish within each military department a board of review to review determinations previously made that covered individuals separated from the Armed Forces have a mental condition not amounting to disability.
Requires the Secretary to ensure that covered individuals receive notification of their right to a separation review by the appropriate Mental Health Discharge Board of Review.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the following: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI); (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA);
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2010, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 4: Requires that amounts made available under the Iraq Security Forces Fund shall be provided as loans subject to full repayment to the U.S. government.

Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2006 to provide that: (1) for each of FY2008-FY2009, the Secretary of Defense shall withhold from obligation and expenditure not less than 20% of the amount made available for the Commanders' Emergency Response Program.
Document 5: This bill directs the Department of Defense (DOD) to: (1) submit to Congress a plan to enhance security of military personnel at DOD installations in the United States.
and (2) implement the plan recommendations within 90 days after enactment of this Act.
Document 6: Prohibits the Attorney General (AG) or any officer or employee of the Department of Justice (DOJ) from initiating a custodial interrogation of, or filing a criminal complaint or indictment against any foreign person detained by the United States.
Requires an annual report from the AG to Congress on the occasions on which direction for action was sought from the President.
Document 7: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI); (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA);
Permits appropriations authorized by this Act for salary, pay, retirement, and other benefits for federal personnel.
Document 8: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI); (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA);
Allows the DNI, with the approval of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2009.
Document 9: Revises the definition of 'entitlement land' for which the Secretary of the Interior is required to make payments to local governments in lieu of taxes.
include federal land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense (DOD) or the Secretary of a military department.
Document 10: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to direct the head of an element of the intelligence community (IC) to provide to the Secretary of Defense any intelligence information obtained by such IC element.
Requires: (1) the Secretary to prescribe a process for expediting an official response to any information submitted.

topic.name: Public Land Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to the management, transfer, and use of public lands in the United States.

Document 1: Reauthorize and make permanent the program for the sale or exchange of public land
Completion of appraisals and satisfaction of other legal requirements
Document 2: Transfer to the Secretary of the Navy administrative jurisdiction over certain public lands
Requires the Secretary of the Navy to prepare an integrated natural resources management plan
Document 3: Take into trust for the benefit of the Samish Indian Nation the specified land
Prohibits the Tribe from conducting gaming on such land
Document 4: Require consultation with federally recognized Indian tribes regarding suicide prevention strategies
Amends the Public Health Service Act
Document 5: Make available moneys received from fines for restoration and public informational activities
Transfers excess funds to the Crime Victims Fund
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to take specified lands into trust for the Santa Ynez Band of Chumash Mission Indians
Prohibits gambling on the land
Document 7: Authorize cooperative agreements for forest and watershed restoration
Activities to treat insect infected forests and reduce hazardous fuels
Document 8: Establish the National Geospatial Technology Administration
Create a National Geospatial Database of U.S. owned or managed lands
Document 9: Facilitate the use of federal public land for hunting and recreational fishing
Requires federal public land management officials to support hunting and fishing opportunities
Document 10: Direct the Department of the Interior to convey federal land to La Paz County
Permit the county to sell or retain land for public recreation and purposes

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving benefits and support services for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to entitle an eligible employee to up to 12 workweeks of leave because of the death of a son or daughter.
Document 2: Prohibits the payment of a performance-based bonus to any officer of the Department of Veterans Affairs unless certain conditions are met.
Document 3: Establishes a program to provide peer support, readjustment, and mental health services to veterans.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to provide necessary outpatient dental services to veterans awarded the Purple Heart.
Document 5: Exempts survivors' and dependents' educational assistance from the 48-month time limit.
Document 6: Increases rates of veterans' benefits for those in need of regular aid and attendance.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to enroll any eligible veteran who applies.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to administer the health care enrollment system.
Document 9: Automatically enrolls certain veterans in the Department of Veterans Affairs medical system.
Document 10: Directs the Department of Defense to provide transportation for veterans with a service-connected disability.

topic.name: Immigration and Border Security
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to immigration enforcement, border security, and employment verification processes in the United States.

Document 1: Sets forth border security and enforcement provisions
Requires that Department of Homeland Security (DHS) personnel check against terrorist watchlists
Document 2: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
Requires employers to verify employee identification and employment eligibility
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security or the Attorney General to adjust to permanent resident status certain alien family members
Document 4: Amends citizenship and residency qualifications for enlistment in the armed forces
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to rescind such adjusted status if the person is separated from the armed forces
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make aliens convicted of sex offenses against minors inadmissible to the United States
Document 6: Denies immunity from the jurisdiction of U.S. courts to a foreign country in any case brought against a foreign country that denied or unreasonably delayed the repatriation of an alien
Document 7: Prohibits appropriated funds for the community oriented policing services program from being used in contravention of section 642(a) of the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act
Document 8: Sets forth border security and enforcement provisions
Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to make the basic employment eligibility confirmation pilot program permanent
Document 9: Provides permanent resident status adjustment for a requesting alien who was on September 10, 2001, the wife, child, or dependent son or daughter of a lawful nonimmigrant alien who died as a result of the September 11, 2001, terrorist attacks
Document 10: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjudicate a visa petition filed by an employer on behalf of an alien with extraordinary ability in the arts

topic.name: Regulatory Framework for Health Products
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at regulating health-related products and services to ensure safety, efficacy, and consumer protection.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow any person to submit an application for approval of a biologics license for a biosimilar
Requires the Secretary to establish a Biosimilars Advisory Committee.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to extend by six months the exclusivity period for a drug or biological product
Applies only to products approved after enactment of this Act for a new indication that is a rare disease or condition.
Document 3: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to prohibit the delivery, distribution, or dispensing of a controlled substance that is a prescription drug over the Internet without a valid prescription
Defines 'online pharmacy' as a person, entity, or Internet site, whether in the United States or abroad, that knowingly or intentionally delivers, distributes, or dispenses a controlled substance.
Document 4: Directs the Consumer Product Safety Commission to issue a rule requiring warning labels for equestrian helmets that are not approved safety helmets
The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention must make grants available for states, local governments, or tax-exempt nonprofit organizations.
Document 5: Requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to establish a consumer product safety standard for window bars

Expresses the sense of Congress that public housing, low-income housing, manufactured housing units, and homes purchased using any loan made, insured, or guaranteed under the National Housing Act should meet a minimal standard.

topic.name: Air Travel Regulations and Fairness
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at ensuring fairness and safety in air travel, including baggage fees, airport security, and passenger rights.

Document 1: Treating Small Airports with Fairness Act of 2016
requires the TSA to conduct security screening at small airports
Document 2: First Bag Flies Free Act
Makes it an unfair or deceptive practice for an air carrier to charge a fee for the first checked bag
Document 3: Baggage Fee Fairness Act of 2011
Requires air carriers to refund passenger baggage fees within 30 days
Document 6: Clear Airfares Act of 2009
Makes it an unfair or deceptive practice for air carriers to sell tickets without displaying all applicable fees
Document 10: Safe Egress in Air Travel Act of 2016
directs the Department of Transportation to issue regulations for minimum dimensions for passenger seats

topic.name: Unemployment Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts related to the extension and reform of unemployment compensation programs in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through January 3, 2013.
Document 6: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before January 1, 2015.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the House of Representatives and the Senate should reconvene on or before December 18, 2013, to take appropriate action to enact this legislation.
Document 9: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before five months after enactment of this Act.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until five months after enactment of this Act requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.

topic.name: Federal Land and Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conveyance, and use of federal lands and resources in the United States.

Document 1: Wallowa Forest Service Compound Conveyance Act - Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey, by quitclaim deed...
Requires the city of Wallowa to use the Compound as a historical and cultural interpretation and education center.
Document 2: Amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act to authorize a state to pay up to 90% of the costs of acquiring land for...
Urges the Chief of the Forest Service and the Director of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to cooperate with state and local authorities...
Document 4: Sets at 2%, for a five-year period, the royalty rate on the quantity or gross value of the output of sodium compounds and related products at the point of shipment to market from federal land.
Document 9: Directs the Administrator of the General Services Administration (GSA) to convey to Miami Dade College of Miami Dade County, Florida, all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the David W. Dyer Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse...
Allows the college to use such property as a cultural and educational complex...

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at supporting and enhancing small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the procurement program for women-owned small businesses
Allows a contracting officer to award a sole source contract to any economically disadvantaged women-owned small business
Document 2: Revises the Small Business Administration's criteria for awarding a grant to a women's business center
Includes the ability of the applicant to provide training and services to a representative number of unmarried women who are both socially and economically disadvantaged
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a rural entrepreneur and microenterprise program
To help low- and moderate-income individuals acquire necessary skills to establish small rural businesses
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to replace the position of breakout procurement representative
Requires representatives to review barriers to small business participation in federal contracting
Document 5: Reauthorizes the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
Increases the set-aside allocation under the STTR from 0.3% to 0.6%
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to authorize the SBA to guarantee the payment of up to $4 billion per fiscal year
Establishes and carries out an early-stage investment program
Document 7: Establishes a Small Business Advisory Board to advise and consult with the CFPB
To provide information on emerging practices of small businesses
Document 8: Amends the Small Business Act to extend the period that a small business may receive developmental assistance
Allows small businesses to participate for an additional two years if they meet certain criteria
Document 9: Requires the Department of Education to award grants to establish or expand STEM secondary schools
Promotes the manufacturing of goods in the United States
Document 10: Reauthorizes both the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program and the Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) Program
Increases the percentages of required agency expenditures with small business concerns under these programs

topic.name: Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, funding, and reforms of the United States Postal Service.

Document 1: requires the United States Postal Service to issue and sell, at a premium, a Multinational Species Conservation Funds Semipostal Stamp
proceeds from the sale of such stamp to be transferred to the United States Fish and Wildlife Service
Document 3: authorizes the Postmaster General to declare up to 12 non-mail delivery days for the Postal Service
requires the Postmaster General to submit to the Board of Governors of the U.S. Postal Service a report that lists when each non-mail delivery day will occur
Document 4: allows the United States Postal Service to accept monetary donations for the funding of plaques
requires the USPS to provide for a plaque after a law has been enacted for such designation
Document 5: directs the U.S. Postal Service to issue a Families of Fallen Heroes Semipostal Stamp
requires 50% of the proceeds from the sale of such stamp to be transferred to the Tragedy Assistance Program for Survivors
Document 9: revises requirements for making a determination as to the necessity for closing or consolidating any postal facility
requires USPS to hold at least three public meetings, located in the area affected by the closing or consolidation
Document 10: amends provisions of federal law relating to the United States Postal Service
establishes the Commission on Postal Reorganization

topic.name: Election Transparency and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, integrity, and accessibility in the electoral process in the United States.

1: prohibit a candidate's authorized committee from employing any of the candidate's relatives in a paid position
limits the interest rate an authorized committee may pay on a loan accepted from the candidate
2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure'
Prescribes disclosure requirements for corporations, labor organizations, and certain other entities
3: establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as the days for the election
polling place hours in the United States for both congressional and presidential elections shall be from 10:00 a.m. EST on Saturday till 6:00 p.m. EST on Sunday
4: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements
establishment of a Senate Fair Elections Fund
6: make it unlawful for a chief state election administration official to take active part in political management
States that the budgetary effects of this Act for compliance with the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
8: makes an individual's unexpired tribal identification document issued by an Indian tribe a valid form of identification
Prohibits states and political subdivisions from eliminating an Indian reservation's sole polling place
9: revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC
10: quadruple the matching rate for contributions to primary election candidates
increase the presidential primary qualifying threshold from $5,000 to $25,000 in 20 states

topic.name: Reproductive Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures related to reproductive health, including abortion regulations, parental notification requirements, and funding restrictions.

Document 1: cost of multilingual services provided or performed by federal agencies
annual reports of federal agency chief financial officers
Document 2: require abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound
provide a medical description of the ultrasound images
Document 3: prohibit the use of any facility of interstate or foreign commerce to teach a person how to commit suicide
imposes a fine and/or prison term for violations
Document 4: deems human life to exist from conception
protect the lives of unborn children
Document 5: require annual state SCHIP reports to include information on access to primary care practitioners
measure the extent to which individuals eligible for child health assistance have access to physicians
Document 6: prohibits federal funding of any affiliate of Planned Parenthood
investigate illegal activity pertaining to fetal tissue products
Document 7: prohibits performance of an abortion on an unemancipated minor without parental notification
establishes a 96-hour waiting period
Document 8: U.S. policy to deny financial resources to the government of Cuba
determine sanctions against investments contributing to Cuba's petroleum resources
Document 9: require child care providers to obtain a comprehensive criminal background check
refuse to employ individuals convicted of crimes against children
Document 10: prohibits federal funds for population control programs
includes any abortion procedure

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: Legislation focused on various aspects of federal law and governance in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the federal judicial code to provide for service of process on foreign citizens or subjects in cases involving defective products.
Conditions jurisdiction for service of process on the fact that the foreign national knew or reasonably should have known that the product would be imported for sale in the United States.
Document 2: Amends federal bankruptcy law to permit an individual debtor to exempt from the property of the estate in bankruptcy the debtor's aggregate interest in firearms.
Declares the date of enactment as the effective date of this Act.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to reduce from 14 years to 7 years the term of office of the members of the Board of Governors.
Requires the President to nominate a person to fill the vacancy within a specified time period.
Document 4: Authorizes FY2014 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2014, for such activities are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 5: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act to establish the Office of the District Attorney for the District of Columbia.
Prohibits the DA from engaging directly or indirectly in private practice.
Document 6: Repeals the elimination of the pension anti-cutback provisions under the Multiemployer Pension Reform Act of 2014.
Amends the federal bankruptcy code to assign first claim priority to pension obligations under ERISA.
Document 7: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to extend its coverage to specified energy commodities.
Directs the CFTC to establish position limits for energy and agricultural commodities.
Document 8: Deems a claim for liability against a qualified entity as a claim against the United States for damages resulting from the use of transportation fuel containing ethanol.
Abrogates U.S. sovereign immunity and makes the United States exclusively liable for such claims.
Document 9: Amends the federal judicial code to establish a two-judge Article III court in the Virgin Islands.
Sets forth provisions respecting assignment of additional judges.
Document 10: Amends the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act to expand the diseases for which individuals exposed to radiation may claim compensation.
Changes the presumption for a finding of cancer compensable under the program to a standard of clear and convincing evidence.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of federal legislative acts addressing various social, environmental, and administrative issues.

Document 1: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Settlement Agreement
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to plan, design, and construct a Regional Water System
Document 2: Amends the DNA Identification Act of 1994
Requires the Federal Bureau of Investigation to issue standards for using Rapid DNA instruments
Document 3: Reauthorizes through FY2019 the programs under titles I-IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act
Expresses the sense of Congress that states and local educational agencies retain rights and responsibilities
Document 4: Reauthorizes through FY2020 the National Transportation Safety Board
Requires the NTSB to investigate derailments involving significant release of hazardous materials
Document 5: Amends the National Housing Act to provide a 50% discount for teachers
Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to inform teachers about the program
Document 6: Authorizes the Navy to convey real property to Escambia County
Requires Escambia County to reimburse the Navy for administrative costs
Document 7: Revises Charter School Programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act
Requires grantees to use 10% of the grant funds to provide technical assistance
Document 8: Amends the Consolidated Security, Disaster Assistance, and Continuing Appropriations Act
Requires DHS to submit a report to Congress
Document 9: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to revise requirements for capitalization grant agreements
Establishes affordability criteria to assist municipalities
Document 10: Makes the Freedom of Information Act applicable to the Smithsonian Institution
Makes this Act applicable to all actions taken by the Board of Regents

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to health care policies, insurance regulations, and mental health services.

Document 1: Permits a health insurance issuer that had in effect health insurance coverage in the group or individual market on any date during 2013 to continue offering that coverage outside of a health care exchange established under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act.
Treats that coverage as a grandfathered health plan for purposes of an individual meeting the requirement to maintain minimum essential health coverage.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award a five-year grant to one department of psychiatry in, or one non-profit entity affiliated with, an accredited medical school in this country to carry out a demonstration program to recruit, train, deploy, and professionally support psychiatric physicians.

Requires participating psychiatric physicians to demonstrate a commitment, beyond the one year of training and two years of deployment, to a career as a specialty care or primary care physician providing mental health services in Indian health programs.
Document 3: Repeals Title I of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (provisions related to health insurance and health coverage expansion) and any amendments to such provisions made by the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010.

Amends the Public Health Service Act to provide that the laws of the state designated by a health insurance issuer (primary state) shall apply to individual health insurance coverage offered by that issuer in the primary state and in any other state (secondary state).
Document 6: Amends the National Housing Act to extend from July 31, 2011 to July 31, 2016, the exemption for critical access hospitals from the limit on patient days customarily assignable to specified categories of care under the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD).
Document 10: Repeals provisions of the Internal Revenue Code (IRC), as added by the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA), that impose fines on large employers who fail to offer their full-time employees the opportunity to enroll in minimum essential health insurance coverage.
Allows a health plan to maintain its status as a grandfathered health plan regardless of any modification to the cost-sharing levels, employer contribution rates, or covered benefits.

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Protections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legal reforms aimed at enhancing due process, accountability, and protections for individuals in different contexts, including military justice, law enforcement, consumer rights, and immigration.

Document 1: Revises requirements relating to an Article 32 investigative hearing under the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ)
Limits the preliminary hearing to the purpose of determining whether there is probable cause to believe an offense has been committed
Document 2: Sets forth the due process rights, including procedures, that shall be afforded a law enforcement officer
Establishes rights of officers before and during questioning, including the right to counsel
Document 4: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act concerning the protection of servicemembers against default judgments
Requires a plaintiff to conduct a diligent and reasonable investigation to determine whether or not the defendant is in military service
Document 6: Prohibits debt settlement providers from providing a debt settlement service or receiving a fee from a consumer without a signed written contract
Declares void and unenforceable a consumer's waiver of any consumer protection or right provided under this Act
Document 9: Requires that counsel at the government's expense in a removal or related appeal proceeding shall be provided only for an alien who is an unaccompanied child or incompetent to represent himself or herself

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Sovereignty and Resource Management
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses U.S. legislative measures related to the recognition of sovereignty, nuclear policy, and resource management, particularly in relation to Israel, nuclear waste, and state-level resource allocation.

Document 1: the United States should recognize Israel's sovereignty and legal right to its lands
transfer the U.S. Embassy from Tel Aviv, Israel, to Jerusalem, Israel
Document 2: require each licensee of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to submit a plan for transfer to spent nuclear fuel dry casks
requires the NRC to approve or disapprove the plan within 90 days
Document 3: Prohibits using funds appropriated to the Department of Defense (DOD) for FY2013 or thereafter
Requires an initial and subsequent annual reports from the Secretaries of Defense and Energy to Congress
Document 4: Conditions the construction by the Secretary of the Interior of the Arkansas Valley Conduit
Requires revenue derived from contracts for the use of Fryingpan-Arkansas project excess capacity
Document 5: Provides for the transfer from the Secretary of the Treasury to the state of Colorado
used by Colorado for conservation, restoration, and protection of land, water, and wildlife resources
Document 6: Amends federal law governing the treatment of oil shale receipts
directs the Secretary of the Treasury to transfer certain moneys deposited in the Treasury to the state of Colorado
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make grants to state housing credit agencies
Requires state housing credit agencies and states to use grants to make subawards
Document 9: is U.S. policy to not recognize the de jure or de facto sovereignty of the Russian Federation over Crimea
Prohibits any federal department or agency from taking any action or extending any assistance that recognizes or implies recognition

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Education and Environment
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving educational leadership, environmental standards, and public health initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states, local educational agencies (LEAs), nonprofit organizations...
Requires the Secretary to award additional competitive grants to states or partnerships for pilot programs that evaluate and promote the incorporation of instructional leadership standards.
Document 5: Prohibits the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from lowering its existing national ambient air quality standards (NAAQS) until at least 85% of counties that are in nonattainment areas have attained the standard.
Requires the EPA, in promulgating a primary or secondary NAAQS for ozone, to only consider a county to be a nonattainment area on the basis of direct air quality monitoring.

topic.name: Environmental Protection and Maritime Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to environmental protection, specifically concerning water bodies, marine ecosystems, and maritime safety regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to make the party responsible for an offshore facility liable for all discharge removal costs and damages.
Makes this Act effective on April 15, 2010.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to include as a purpose to achieve the goals established in the Great Lakes Restoration Initiative Action Plan.
Expands the duties of the Great Lakes Program Office to include coordinating with the Great Lakes Interagency Task Force.
Document 4: Requires the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to establish a coordinated national ocean exploration program.
Authorizes appropriations for the program for FY2008-FY2017.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt from the harbor maintenance tax commercial cargo loaded at a port in the U.S. mainland.
Defines the 'Great Lakes Saint Lawrence Seaway System' as the waterway encompassing the five Great Lakes.
Document 9: Implements the Agreement on Port State Measures to Prevent, Deter and Eliminate Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated Fishing.
Authorizes the Secretary of Commerce to designate and publicize ports of entry for foreign vessels.
